content:
- Ralph task switch completed and updated current_tasks/current_tasks_done/task_history.
- No task-file edits were needed at closeout; the recurring meta-task must stay <passes>meta-task</passes>.
- Next step is committing all Ralph bookkeeping with explicit gate evidence, then pushing.

- Commit d8ba0f0 created successfully after the post-commit docs publish hook completed.
- The hook reported no extra docs changes to commit/push.
- Running explicit git push now as the final required closeout step.

- Read the existing progress log for Choose-Task and inspected .ralph/current_tasks.md plus .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Current active summary appears to contain only the recurring meta-task in story-rust-system-harness; done summary shows bug backlog entries are marked passes=true.
- Checking raw current_tasks.md length/content now to confirm there are zero unblocked failing tasks before selecting the meta-task path and writing it to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Confirmed .ralph/current_tasks.md contains exactly one active task: the recurring meta-task .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md.
- Re-checked task selection rules against current_tasks_done: no unblocked failing bug tasks or other failing tasks remain active, so meta-task becomes mandatory.
- Wrote the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and am stopping immediately as instructed.

- Loaded prior progress and active task; current task is the recurring meta-task 18 deep skeptical review.
- The task file already ends with NOW EXECUTE and the pass-8 checklist items are already checked, so this turn is executing/closing rather than replanning.
- Next I need to inspect git/task/model state carefully and finish any remaining closeout steps without violating the meta-task rule that <passes> stays meta-task.

- Verified the recurring meta-task is already closed out in git: HEAD and origin/master are both at 07d4817 (`task finished 18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review: sync final Ralph bookkeeping`).
- `.ralph/model.txt` is back to `normal_high`, and the task file still correctly keeps `<passes>meta-task</passes>` with the pass-8 section ending in NOW EXECUTE.
- Remaining working tree changes are only fresh Ralph bookkeeping from this session / task selection (`current_task.txt`, regenerated summaries, appended progress`), so I am not creating a redundant extra task-finished commit.

- Loaded progress log and current task pointer.
- Active task still points at `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`; inspecting whether it is in planning, verification, or execution state.
- Working tree currently has Ralph bookkeeping changes only: `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, `.ralph/progress/136.jsonl`, and untracked `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Re-read the recurring meta-task file and confirmed it is already in `NOW EXECUTE` state with the pass-8 checklist fully checked.
- Verified HEAD and origin/master are already at `07d4817`, which is a pushed `task finished 18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review: sync final Ralph bookkeeping` commit.
- This session produced only fresh Ralph bookkeeping (`current_task.txt`, regenerated summaries, appended progress). I am stopping without a redundant duplicate task-finished commit because the task was already completed and pushed.

- Read prior Ralph progress and confirmed the active task pointer resolves to `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- About to inspect the task file contents to determine whether the required action is planning, skeptical verification, or straight execution.
- Need to follow the task state strictly; if it is still in planning state I should write the detailed plan and quit immediately.

- Read the active task file and found it already in `NOW EXECUTE` state, with the pass-8 checklist items shown as checked.
- This means I should not re-plan; the remaining question is whether any actual execution/closeout is still pending or whether the pointer is stale after a completed run.
- Inspecting git state, upstream parity, and Ralph current-task bookkeeping before deciding whether any further action is legitimate.

- Verified the active recurring meta-task is already fully executed in the repository: task file remains at `NOW EXECUTE` with the pass-8 checklist checked, and HEAD==origin/master at commit `07d4817` (`task finished 18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review: sync final Ralph bookkeeping`).
- The only local modifications are fresh Ralph bookkeeping from this inspection session (`.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, `.ralph/progress/136.jsonl`, untracked `.ralph/current_task.txt`). No product/code/docs work remains for this task.
- Stopping without creating a duplicate task-finished commit; the current-task pointer appears stale or reselected after completion. If resumed later, switch to a different task instead of re-running pass-8.

- Investigated user question about why `postgres_dsn` is a String instead of a typed struct.
- Confirmed the codebase already has typed conninfo in `src/pginfo/conninfo.rs` as `PgConnInfo` with parse/render helpers, and most runtime/process/HA paths use it.
- Found `postgres_dsn` only in `PgInfoWorkerCtx` and pginfo polling path; runtime builds it with `local_postgres_dsn()` as a raw formatted conninfo string and passes it straight to `tokio_postgres::connect`.
- Conclusion: this is not a separate richer model; it is an untyped last-mile string that likely survived because `tokio_postgres::connect` accepts `&str`. Next time, if asked to change it, refactor pginfo worker ctx to hold `PgConnInfo` and render at the connect boundary.

- Refactored pginfo worker path to carry typed `PgConnInfo` instead of raw `postgres_dsn` strings.
- Updated `PgInfoWorkerCtx`, `pginfo::query::poll_once`, runtime local conn builder, worker contract test, and pginfo worker tests.
- Kept string rendering only at the `tokio_postgres::connect` boundary via `render_pg_conninfo`.
- Verified with sequential tests after correcting my own parallel cargo-test contention issue: `cargo test step_once_contracts_are_callable -- --nocapture`, `cargo test runtime_uses_role_specific_users_for_dsn_clone_and_rewind -- --nocapture`, and `cargo test pginfo -- --nocapture` all passed.

- Investigated why `parse_pg_conninfo` exists and traced all uses with `rg`.
- Confirmed only one non-test direct call exists: `src/postgres_managed_conf.rs` inside `parse_managed_primary_conninfo`, which is used by `read_existing_replica_start_intent` when reconstructing managed replica state from `pgtm.postgresql.conf` on disk.
- Verified normal construction paths already use typed `PgConnInfo` directly from runtime/HA/process code; parsing is only for ingesting persisted `primary_conninfo` text back into typed state. Runtime startup currently only needs `is_some()`, but HA process dispatch can return the full parsed `ManagedPostgresStartIntent`, so the parser is still serving a real recovery/resume path.

- User asked how to find more typed-vs-untyped drift cases and requested adding a generic heuristic to recurring meta-task 18.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md` with a new representation-integrity review section focused on canonical typed models vs ad-hoc primitive/internal encodings across subsystem boundaries.
- Wording was kept generic on purpose: it instructs review from domain concepts and boundary crossings rather than from seeded examples.

- Ran a live representation-drift scan after adding the generic heuristic to task 18.
- The method worked: strongest new issue found is untyped network endpoints carried as raw `String`/`Vec<String>` across config/runtime/DCS/harness, with parsing deferred to scattered use sites.
- Created bug task `.ralph/tasks/bugs/typed-network-endpoints-instead-of-raw-strings.md`.
- Also reviewed likely false positives: managed primary conninfo parser duplication looks boundary-specific/intentional because it extends libpq conninfo with managed passfile semantics; `PostgresConnIdentityConfig` looks like a deliberate partial config projection, not an accidental duplicate of `PgConnInfo`.

- Investigated user concerns about broader desired architecture versus current code.
- Confirmed `ManagedPostgresStartIntent` is only the startup-mode subset: primary vs replica vs recovery, with replica/recovery carrying `PgConnInfo`, standby auth, and optional slot; it is not the full `ManagedPostgresConf`.
- Confirmed managed config materialization happens in `materialize_managed_postgres_config` before StartPostgres jobs; there is no separate continuous HA reconciliation loop rewriting config on every watcher tick.
- Current code does not appear to require any JSON sidecar in PGDATA: the only persisted managed artifacts are the rendered config/signal/passfile/TLS files, and the parse-back path exists solely because startup/dispatch sometimes reconstruct from those files.

- Executed startup progress read via .ralph/progress_read.sh; no visible output in this environment/session.
- Searched `.ralph/progress/*.jsonl` for `primary_conninfo`, `parse_pg_conninfo`, `parse_managed_primary_conninfo`, and disk-read language.
- Found matching mentions in `.ralph/progress/105.jsonl`, `.ralph/progress/136.jsonl`, `.ralph/progress/133.jsonl`, `.ralph/progress/113.jsonl`, `.ralph/progress/132.jsonl`, `.ralph/progress/65.jsonl`, and `.ralph/progress/56.jsonl`.
- Key strongest evidence is in `136.jsonl` that `parse_managed_primary_conninfo` is used by `read_existing_replica_start_intent` to reconstruct managed replica state from `pgtm.postgresql.conf` on disk, which directly supports parse-back behavior.
- Next: report matching entries and conclusion to the user.

- Searched src/tests/docs/task history for startup resume + managed replica state behavior around primary_conninfo.
- Inspected startup planner flow in runtime and HA dispatch; both call read_existing_replica_start_intent() for managed on-disk state.
- Confirmed startup planner policy: DCS topology is authoritative for resume role; on-disk managed replica state is used for consistency/continuation only.
- Confirmed managed config helper currently parses full `primary_conninfo` (including passfile/auth signal) from `pgtm.postgresql.conf` when reconstructing start intent.
- Identified tests that show either artifact-detection behavior (ignore/require DCS authority) or full round-trip behavior from managed `.conf`.
- Checked authoritative docs/tasks showing intent that startup should avoid trusting unmanaged `postgresql.auto.conf` and legacy raw config side effects.

- Ran git history and current-file inspection for startup planning and managed replica-state handling.
- Confirmed DCS-authoritative behavior for startup intent: resume/clone path is gated by healthy DCS primary evidence.
- Confirmed read_existing_replica_start_intent was introduced to parse managed pgtm.postgresql.conf + managed signal files, while docs/tests now frame old local files as consistency signals only.
- Gathered commit references around 2ed1a59 and 07349fc, plus current node/process_dispatch/postgres_managed implementations and tests.

- Investigated Ralph history for managed `.conf` migration and startup-intent design, focusing on whether the project intentionally needed to parse managed PostgreSQL config back from disk into typed state.
- Strong evidence came from authoritative-managed-postgres-config tasks 01/02/03, the standby-auth bug task, and progress/136.jsonl. The design explicitly moved authority away from postgres-owned recovery files and backup JSON sidecars, toward pgtuskmaster-authored artifacts in PGDATA.
- Key conclusion: a separate persisted state artifact beyond rendered managed files was repeatedly *not* chosen. The only persisted managed artifacts intended were the rendered files themselves (`pgtm.postgresql.conf`, signal files, later passfile/TLS/HBA/ident files). Reading/parsing back from disk existed for resume/rejoin cases, especially startup without DCS leader or shared HA/runtime reconstruction of previously managed replica state.
- Next if resumed: if the user asks for stronger proof, inspect git commits around the authoritative-managed-config story implementation and correlate with current source helpers like `read_existing_replica_start_intent`.

- Spawned 1 explorer and 5 explore_spark subagents to inspect `.ralph/progress/*.jsonl`, `.ralph/archive/`, and task history for why managed startup state was ever parsed back from disk.
- Synthesis: history shows parse-back was a deliberate resume/rejoin design choice for reconstructing previously managed replica intent from pgtuskmaster-owned artifacts in PGDATA when DCS was absent/insufficient, but not a PostgreSQL requirement and not evidence that a full persisted typed sidecar is necessary.
- Strong evidence found that persisted managed startup artifacts in PGDATA are required; weak/no evidence found that a JSON/YAML state file is required. A small persisted marker was only raised historically as a possibility to verify.
- Created detailed follow-up task at `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/task-remove-managed-conf-parseback-and-rederive-start-intent.md` to remove parse-back, re-derive start intent from DCS/config/local facts, and only introduce a tiny persisted marker if proven necessary by code/tests.

- Read the current functional HA flow to refine the task against the actual architecture rather than a generic startup plan.
- Confirmed `src/ha/decide.rs` and `src/ha/lower.rs` are already clean: they emit `WaitForPostgres { start_requested, leader_member_id }` and lower to `PostgresEffect::Start` without parsing startup files.
- Confirmed the remaining parse-back leak is specifically in `src/ha/process_dispatch.rs` when `HaAction::StartPostgres` materializes into a `ManagedPostgresStartIntent`: leader-derived DCS path is clean, fallback path calls `read_existing_replica_start_intent` -> `parse_managed_primary_conninfo` -> `parse_pg_conninfo`.
- Updated the task file to explicitly preserve the functional HA decision/apply architecture and to target the parse-back removal at the start-intent materialization boundary instead.

- User asked to move every task file with `<passes>true</passes>` out of `.ralph/tasks` into `.ralph/archive/archive_tasks`.
- I confirmed the task-file header format and found 113 matching markdown task files; existing `.ralph/archive/archived_tasks` is present but empty, while the user-requested `.ralph/archive/archive_tasks` did not exist.
- Next I am creating `.ralph/archive/archive_tasks`, moving the 113 completed task files there with their relative folder structure preserved, and verifying that `.ralph/tasks` no longer contains any passes=true task files.

- Created `.ralph/archive/archive_tasks` because the user explicitly asked for that path; the repo also has an existing but empty `.ralph/archive/archived_tasks` directory.
- Moved 113 markdown task files whose header contains `<passes>true</passes>` from `.ralph/tasks` into `.ralph/archive/archive_tasks`, preserving their relative subdirectory structure.
- Verified the move: `.ralph/tasks` now has 0 remaining passes=true task markdown files, and `.ralph/archive/archive_tasks` now contains 113 passes=true task markdown files.

- User asked what to do with the leftover task files after archiving all passes=true tasks, and to remove empty directories afterward.
- I am enumerating the remaining task files and directories first so I can recommend whether they belong in an existing story, one new shared story, or two new stories before making any structural cleanup.

- Inspected the three remaining task files after archiving completed work.
- Best-fit assessment: the managed-conf parse-back task belongs under the authoritative managed postgres config story; the typed network endpoints bug is broad runtime/config/DCS boundary hygiene and fits best either as a bug under the remaining active surface or a future dedicated representation-integrity story, but it does not naturally belong to the managed-conf story.
- Removing only directories that are currently empty: 12 empty `.ralph/tasks/story-*` directories and the empty `.ralph/archive/archived_tasks` directory.

- User rejected the leftover placement, specifically that `story-rust-system-harness` is too broad and that the recurring meta-task should not live in the same story bucket as implementation work.
- I am restructuring the remaining live task tree into purpose-specific directories: one for the managed-start-intent architecture task, one separate meta-task directory for recurring deep review, and one better-scoped story bucket for the representation-integrity bug instead of leaving the taxonomy mismatched.

- Moved the remaining live files into clearer buckets:
- `task-remove-managed-conf-parseback-and-rederive-start-intent.md` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-managed-start-intent-architecture/`
- `typed-network-endpoints-instead-of-raw-strings.md` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-representation-integrity/`
- `18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md` -> `.ralph/tasks/meta-recurring-review/`
- Next I am deleting the now-empty legacy directories `bugs` and `story-rust-system-harness` and verifying the final live task tree.

- User wants the representation-integrity item moved back under `bugs` and the recurring meta task renamed to a much shorter filename without the `18-` prefix.
- I am verifying the current live task tree first because the previous turn was interrupted, then I will apply those naming/location fixes and clean up any empty directories created by the move.

- Restored `typed-network-endpoints-instead-of-raw-strings.md` to `.ralph/tasks/bugs/` per user request.
- Renamed the recurring meta-task file from `18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md` to the shorter `.ralph/tasks/meta-recurring-review/deep-review.md`.
- Removing the now-empty `.ralph/tasks/story-representation-integrity` directory and then verifying the final live task tree.

- User asked how docs work currently and, in theory, what would need deletion if all docs were rewritten.
- Inspected docs pipeline: mdBook source is `docs/src` (`docs/book.toml` sets `src = "src"`), `make docs-build` builds to `docs/book`, `make docs-serve` serves it, `scripts/publish-docs.sh` lints/builds and copies `docs/book` into sibling repo `../pgtuskmaster-docs/dist` and pushes.
- Identified docs-specific support files: `docs/book.toml`, `docs/mermaid.min.js`, `docs/mermaid-init.js`, `tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh`, `tools/docs-scope-map.py`, `tools/docs-claim-ledger.py`, `tools/docs-mermaid-lint.mjs`, mdBook installer scripts, publish script, and README links into `docs/src`.
- Key conclusion forming: rewriting docs does not require deleting Rust `src/*.rs` files; the authored docs to replace are the markdown files under `docs/src/`, plus possibly docs tooling only if the new docs system abandons mdBook entirely.

- User asked for a new skill under .agents/skills that lets the model ask another model via opencode CLI.
- Read progress log, loaded skill-creator guidance, inspected local .agents/skills structure, and verified opencode is installed at ~/.bun/bin/opencode.
- Confirmed current opencode docs/config details from official docs: OPENCODE_CONFIG custom path, OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT inline override, permission syntax now preferred over deprecated tools booleans, and OpenRouter provider/model config examples.
- Verified local OpenRouter auth exists with `opencode auth list` and successfully ran a non-interactive OpenRouter test via `opencode run --format json` using an inline config with permissions denied and model `openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2`.
- Next: create the new skill directory with SKILL.md + opencode.json + stdin wrapper, then test the wrapper using the Kimi K2 thinking model through OpenRouter.

- Added new skill at .agents/skills/opencode-openrouter-ask with SKILL.md, bundled opencode.json, and executable query-opencode.sh wrapper.
- Config pins default model alias `openrouter/kimi-k2-thinking`, maps it to OpenRouter model `moonshotai/kimi-k2-thinking`, enables only OpenRouter, and denies all tool permissions with `"*": "deny"`.
- Wrapper reads the entire prompt from stdin, errors on empty stdin, honors OPENCODE_MODEL/OPENCODE_FORMAT/OPENCODE_THINKING/OPENCODE_ATTACH/OPENCODE_DIR overrides, and otherwise streams raw opencode output.
- Tested the real shipped wrapper end-to-end with stdin heredoc input; opencode responded successfully with `SKILL_SCRIPT_OK`. Also validated resolved config with `OPENCODE_CONFIG=.agents/skills/opencode-openrouter-ask/opencode.json opencode debug config`.
- If resumed later: possible nice-to-have is adding UI metadata under agents/openai.yaml, but current repo skills do not use that pattern and the user did not ask for it.

- Started this turn by reading the progress log and listing all docs pages under docs/src.
- User explicitly asked to use the opencode-openrouter-ask skill, so I am selecting one markdown page and will replace the full file contents with an opencode-produced phrasing improvement.
- Next I am reading a couple candidate pages, choosing one with straightforward prose, then I will send the entire page plus a phrasing-only instruction through the skill wrapper.

- Chose docs/src/start-here/problem.md as the target because it is a self-contained prose page with stable structure.
- Submitted the full page text to the opencode-openrouter-ask wrapper with a strict phrasing-only instruction: preserve heading structure, markdown, links, inline code, and substance, and return only the rewritten markdown.
- Next I will capture the model output, replace the entire markdown file contents with it, then verify the diff and resulting page text.

- The opencode wrapper invocation started and showed the model build banner but has not yet returned generated markdown.
- I am checking whether the process is still active or stalled so I can finish the user-requested full-file replacement without guessing.
- If it is merely slow, I will keep waiting; if it is hung, I will rerun the same skill invocation in a more deterministic way while still sending the full markdown page through stdin.

- Received the full rewritten markdown back from the opencode-openrouter-ask wrapper after a slow OpenRouter response.
- Replaced docs/src/start-here/problem.md wholesale with the model output, then normalized the new Unicode dashes back to ASCII to stay aligned with repo editing conventions.
- Final step is verifying the exact diff and resulting file contents before reporting back to the user.
